title: Column width not accurate
author: fbele
body:
Hi,
If I want to define the column width, I need to add to almost every width a value of 0.71. So if I want a width of 12, I need actually to define it like `column.width = 12.71`.
Only when it comes to the width of less then 1, this addition actually varies.

Does anybody have an experience, what could cause such a behavior?

Thanks

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body: @fbele may I please You to share with code?

comments.author: fbele
comments.body:
Hi,

First, I would like to point out, that I am using a **browser** version with Angular 7.

I just have a normal worksheet:

`const worksheet = workbook.addWorksheet('Sheet', {views: [{}]);`

And then I am adding my columns like following:

`
        worksheet.columns = [
            { key: 'A', width: 3.71 },
            { key: 'B', width: 5.42 },
            { key: 'C', width: 74.71 },
            { key: 'D', width: 22.71 },
            { key: 'E', width: 16.57 },
            { key: 'F', width: 12.71 },
            { key: 'G', width: 1 },
            { key: 'H', width: 22.71 },
            { key: 'I', width: 1 },
            { key: 'J', width: 50.71 }
        ];
`

The width values here have mostly (not always) added value of 0.71 value to the actual wanted width value.
For the width values that are less then 1, then I actually added a value of 0.42.
For the width above 74 value I added just 0.57 to it.
So this is not constant.

Here is an example of three columns in my project, which is the result of the width values configured above:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33025896/53045688-8a315500-348e-11e9-94a3-133530d5d762.png)

comments.author: jkRaccoon
comments.body: same on me. mine is Vuejs

comments.author: werkilla
comments.body: Thanks, I'm having the same problem in node with the stream workbook writer.  I thought my width values were just not taking but this helped me.

comments.author: yktakaha4
comments.body:
The same thing happened in my environment (Excel for Mac, Version 16.41, In Japanese, node v12.14.1),
The solution was to multiply the width of each column by `1.17` instead of adding the value.

```
[...new Array(ws.columnCount)].map((_, index) => ws.getColumn(index + 1)).forEach((column) => column.width = (column.width ?? 0) * 1.17)
```

It seemed that the size of the width(幅) corresponding to the pixel(ピクセル) changed before and after saving by exceljs.
(Is there a phenomenon such as loss of column width units due to saving?)

[original]
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20282867/94178278-dac42c00-fed5-11ea-9797-c93a181a5766.png)

[after saving(* 1.17)]
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20282867/94178293-def04980-fed5-11ea-8ed2-cfbc310f528c.png)

[after saving(do nothing)]
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20282867/94178557-368eb500-fed6-11ea-9603-b32ad26e0895.png)

comments.author: fbele
comments.body:
> no news?

nope, unfortunately not. and as you see also not much attantion was put to.
it's also kind of baffeling that us few could experienced such a bug.
but back then I have given up on exceljs so I don't know if the bug was fixed in the newer releases.

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
I suppose it is dependent on which OS is used while opening a created file, or which spreadsheet program&version, or used system DPI, or default font.

1. Could I please share with the wrong file and information which OS and App you use?
2. Did you tested it during some different App or OS?

---
_I'm contributing ExcelJS just for fun. If want you to know something more go into #1500_

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
I mean that, which software version are you using to open xlsx (for me it is Microsoft Macintosh Excel v16). It is essential because xlsx format keeps width as a character length, that occurs different width through different OS (dpi changed) or when different default font has been set. More information could be found on the MS Excel documentation page: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/office/troubleshoot/excel/determine-column-widths

However, I don't know if I understand your issue. Have you possibility to write a simple test code which currently fails? This issue will be resolved if the test becomes to pass.


---
_I'm contributing ExcelJS just for fun. If want you to know something more go into #1500_

comments.author: ctheiss
comments.body:
I'm also getting this issue; here's some output to help with debugging.

**OS version**
Edition	Windows 10 Home
Version	22H2
Installed on	‎2020-‎07-‎02
OS build	19045.2846
Experience	Windows Feature Experience Pack 120.2212.4190.0

**Environment**
WSL version 2
Ubuntu 18.04.5 LTS
Node v16.19.1
ExcelJS 4.3.0
Excel 2021 MSO (Version 2303 Build 16.0.16227.20202) 64-bit

**Test file**
```js
const ExcelJS = require('exceljs');

const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
const sheet = workbook.addWorksheet('Test');
sheet.columns = [{ header:'Data', width:10 }];

sheet.addRow([ 50 ]);
sheet.addRow([ 80 ]);

workbook.xlsx.writeFile('test.xlsx');
```

**Output**
![Screenshot 2023-05-01 102219](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/167414/235468017-1211352d-c2c7-421c-bf48-501bb76d9925.png)

I've also uploaded the resultant file: 
[test.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/11365918/test.xlsx)

comments.author: ctheiss
comments.body:
This is perhaps a difference between how the width is specified in the OpenXML and how it's interpreted by Excel.

I found a post on the OpenXML MSDN forum that is very similar to this issue: https://social.msdn.microsoft.com/Forums/sqlserver/en-US/3202fa49-0bae-4846-a1b5-fa29b7f64576/setting-column-width-in-excel?forum=oxmlsdk

This led me to the following explanation of width: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/office/developer/office-2010/cc802410(v=office.14)?redirectedfrom=MSDN

> Column width measured as the number of characters of the maximum digit width of the numbers 0, 1, 2, …, 9 as rendered in the normal style's font. There are 4 pixels of margin padding (two on each side), plus 1 pixel padding for the gridlines.
>
>width = Truncate([{Number of Characters} * {Maximum Digit Width} + {5 pixel padding}]/{Maximum Digit Width}*256)/256
>
>[Example: Using the Calibri font as an example, the maximum digit width of 11 point font size is 7 pixels (at 96 dpi). In fact, each digit is the same width for this font. Therefore, if the cell width is 8 characters wide, the value of this attribute must be Truncate([8*7+5]/7*256)/256 = 8.7109375. end example]

So there's the 0.71.

It seems that, in order to fix this issue, we would have to:
1. find all (font, size) pairs used in a column
2. render the digits 0-9 in each pair, and find the max width in pixels
3. add 5/(max width) to the column width

Or, just assume Calibri 11 is used and add 5/7.
